close_reason: Schema changes implemented and migration applied. REVIEW_REMINDER added to NotificationType enum, lastReviewedAt DateTime? added to User model, reviewReminderEnabled/reviewReminderTime added to userPreferencesSchema. Migration 20260309024845_add_review_reminder_schema committed.
description:
Add two schema changes required by the at-risk review feature and the push notification reminder, then run a Prisma migration.

FILE: prisma/schema.prisma

CHANGE 1 — NotificationType enum
Add REVIEW_REMINDER to the existing enum so the daily cron can record that a reminder was sent and the dedup query can filter on it:

  enum NotificationType {
    ITEM_EXPIRING
    ITEM_EXPIRED
    ITEM_STATUS_CHANGED
    REVIEW_REMINDER   // new
    SYSTEM
  }

CHANGE 2 — User model
Add a nullable lastReviewedAt DateTime field to the User model (after the existing 'onboarded' field, before the closing @@unique):

  model User {
    ...
    onboarded Boolean @default(false)
    lastReviewedAt DateTime?   // new
    ...
  }

FILE: src/types/user-preferences.ts

CHANGE 3 — userPreferencesSchema
Add two new preference keys inside the userPreferencesSchema z.object({...}) block, below the existing emailFrequency field:

  reviewReminderEnabled: z.boolean().default(true),
  reviewReminderTime: z.string().regex(/^\d{2}:\d{2}$/).default('09:00'),

The reviewReminderTime field is added now so the schema is stable, but the time-picker UI is out of scope for this issue. The reviewReminderEnabled key is used by the Settings UI (broccoli-dashboard-7kc) and the cron script (broccoli-dashboard-gcy).

IMPORTANT: The userPreferencesSchema uses a dynamic pattern — AI provider fields are generated via a loop at the top of the file. The new fields are static and should be added to the z.object({}) literal below the existing emailFrequency field. Because the schema drives PreferenceKey (via keyof), UserSettingSwitchCard will automatically accept 'reviewReminderEnabled' as a valid settingKey after this change.

MIGRATION
Run: npx prisma migrate dev --name add-review-reminder-schema
Verify the generated migration file is committed to git. The migration will add one column to the users table and one value to the NotificationType enum. No data backfill is needed.
title: Schema changes: REVIEW_REMINDER notification type + lastReviewedAt on User

description: The plan notes this as a future tradeoff (AT_RISK_REVIEW_PLAN.md L661). Currently mutations fire immediately per swipe/button tap. A future option is to accumulate all ReviewResult decisions in local state and flush them in a single batch (e.g. Promise.all of updateItem calls) when the user reaches the AtRiskReviewComplete screen. Pros: fewer round-trips, cleaner optimistic UX. Cons: if the user closes mid-deck, no partial progress is saved — items remain at-risk and will re-appear next session (which is arguably acceptable). Only worth doing if network performance is a concern in practice. Linked to epic broccoli-dashboard-5ry.
title: AtRiskReview: consider batching card mutations on session complete instead of per-swipe

close_reason: Updated AT_RISK_REVIEW_PLAN.md: fixed markReviewComplete to use toDateString() not toISOString() (line ~156), and fixed ReviewDecision 'eaten' variant to match implementation — { type: 'eaten' } with no percentConsumed field (line ~169). No code changes needed.
description:
Two inconsistencies between AT_RISK_REVIEW_PLAN.md and the actual implementation. Both are already correct in code — the plan doc needs updating.

INCONSISTENCY 1 — markComplete localStorage format (line 156):
  Plan says: localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, new Date().toISOString())
  Impl uses:  localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, new Date().toDateString())
  Why it matters: shouldShowReview() compares stored !== new Date().toDateString(). Storing toISOString() would never match toDateString(), so the once-per-day gate would never engage. The implementation is correct; the plan is wrong.
  Fix: Change line 156 to: localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, new Date().toDateString())

INCONSISTENCY 2 — ReviewDecision 'eaten' variant (lines 169-173):
  Plan says: { type: 'eaten'; percentConsumed: 100 }
  Impl uses:  { type: 'eaten' }  (no percentConsumed field — it's inferred as 100 inside advance())
  Why it matters: AtRiskReviewCard.tsx onDecision({ type: 'eaten' }) will typecheck against the hook's actual type, not the plan's. If someone reads the plan and adds percentConsumed to a call site, it will cause a TS error.
  Fix: Update plan lines 169-173 to match the implemented type union.

ACTION: Update AT_RISK_REVIEW_PLAN.md lines 155-156 and 169-173 to match the implementation. No code changes needed.
title: AtRiskReview: fix plan inconsistency — markComplete should store toDateString not toISOString

close_reason: Implemented both hooks. useAtRiskReview: queue initialised once from getAtRiskItems query, advance() fires full-object updateItem mutation, invalidates getItems on success, exposes queue/currentItem/results/counts. useAtRiskReviewSession: localStorage once-per-day gate, markComplete writes localStorage + calls markReviewComplete mutation, forceOpen bypass for push deep-link. Both pass tsc --noEmit.
description:
Create the two client-side hooks that power the review session logic. Both hooks live in src/components/core/AtRiskReview/ (create this directory — it does not exist yet).

--- HOOK 1: useAtRiskReview.ts ---

TYPES (define at top of file, also exported for use by other components):

  import type { ItemStatusType } from '@/generated/prisma/client';
  import type { CombinedItemType } from '@/components/core/Inventory';

  export type ReviewDecision =
    | { type: 'eaten' }
    | { type: 'discarded' }
    | { type: 'updated'; percentConsumed: number; status?: ItemStatusType }
    | { type: 'skipped' };

  export interface ReviewResult {
    itemId: number;
    decision: ReviewDecision;
  }

RESPONSIBILITIES:
- Calls api.item.getAtRiskItems.useQuery() (added in broccoli-dashboard-k5p).
- Filters results client-side: include BAD and OLD items always; include FRESH items only if getDaysUntilExpiration(item) <= 3. Import getDaysUntilExpiration from '@/utils/expiration'.
- Initialises queue: CombinedItemType[] from the filtered result (only set once on first load — do NOT reset queue on every query refetch, or cards in progress would disappear).
- Maintains results: ReviewResult[] (decisions taken so far).
- Exposes advance(itemId: number, decision: ReviewDecision): removes the item from the queue, appends to results, fires the correct tRPC mutation.

CRITICAL — updateItem mutation constraint:
The existing api.item.updateItem mutation (src/types/item.ts) requires ALL fields: id, name, price, quantity, groceryTripId, importId, itemTypes, percentConsumed, status. It is NOT a partial update. When advance() fires for 'eaten' or 'discarded' or 'updated', you must spread the full item fields:

  api.item.updateItem.useMutation({...}).mutate({
    id: item.id,
    name: item.name,
    description: item.description ?? '',
    price: String(item.price),
    quantity: item.quantity,
    unit: item.unit ?? '',
    groceryTripId: item.groceryTripId,
    importId: item.importId ?? '',
    itemTypes: item.itemTypes.map(t => ({ id: t.id })),
    percentConsumed: decision.type === 'eaten' ? 100 : (decision.type === 'updated' ? decision.percentConsumed : item.percentConsumed),
    status: decision.type === 'eaten' ? 'EATEN' : decision.type === 'discarded' ? 'DISCARDED' : (decision.type === 'updated' && decision.status ? decision.status : item.status),
  });

For 'skipped': no mutation, just pop the queue.

On mutation success: call utils.item.getItems.invalidate() so the Inventory list (which uses getItems) stays in sync. Do NOT invalidate getAtRiskItems — the queue is managed in local state.

RETURNED VALUES:
  {
    queue: CombinedItemType[],       // items not yet decided
    results: ReviewResult[],          // completed decisions
    currentItem: CombinedItemType | undefined,  // queue[0]
    advance: (itemId: number, decision: ReviewDecision) => void,
    isLoading: boolean,               // query is pending
    isEmpty: boolean,                 // queue.length === 0 (and not loading)
    eatenCount: number,
    discardedCount: number,
    skippedCount: number,
  }

--- HOOK 2: useAtRiskReviewSession.ts ---

CONSTANTS:
  const STORAGE_KEY = 'broccoli:atRiskReview:lastCompletedDate';

LOGIC:
  shouldShowReview(): boolean
    - Reads localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY)
    - Returns true if null/empty OR if the stored date string does not match new Date().toDateString()
    - Returns false if the stored date matches today (already reviewed today on this device)

  markComplete(): void
    - Writes new Date().toISOString() to localStorage under STORAGE_KEY
    - Also calls api.user.markReviewComplete.mutate() to persist cross-device

  forceOpen state: boolean
    - useState(false), exposed as { forceOpen, setForceOpen }
    - When true, bypasses the shouldShowReview() gate (used by the push notification deep-link)

RETURNED VALUES:
  {
    shouldShow: boolean,         // shouldShowReview() || forceOpen
    markComplete: () => void,
    forceOpen: boolean,
    setForceOpen: (v: boolean) => void,
  }

DEPENDS ON: broccoli-dashboard-k5p (for api.item.getAtRiskItems and api.user.markReviewComplete).
title: useAtRiskReview + useAtRiskReviewSession hooks

description: Epic covering the full mobile swipe-to-review card deck experience for at-risk inventory items (BAD, OLD, FRESH expiring within 3 days), plus a daily push notification reminder that deep-links into the deck. Users are prompted once per day on mobile to swipe through at-risk items and log whether each was eaten, discarded, or partially consumed — similar to how Tinder lets you review profiles via a swipable card stack. The feature is gated to mobile (isMobile === true) and fires at most once per calendar day via a localStorage fast-path cache backed by a lastReviewedAt DB field. All required dependencies (framer-motion, vaul, canvas-confetti, nuqs, web-push) are already installed. Implementation plan: AT_RISK_REVIEW_PLAN.md
title: At-risk items review experience

description:
The at-risk review deck components are all built but the feature is NOT yet accessible to any user. This tracks the minimum path to a first working end-to-end experience.

WHAT IS BUILT (closed issues):
- broccoli-dashboard-479: useAtRiskReview + useAtRiskReviewSession hooks
- broccoli-dashboard-z4y: AtRiskReviewCard
- broccoli-dashboard-le3: AtRiskReviewCardDetails
- broccoli-dashboard-rqy: AtRiskReviewCardOverlay
- broccoli-dashboard-8d4: AtRiskReviewDeck

- broccoli-dashboard-k5p: tRPC queries (getAtRiskItems + markReviewComplete)
- broccoli-dashboard-gcy: Cron script (send-review-reminders.ts - daily push at 9 AM)

WHAT IS MISSING (open - blocks shipping):
1. broccoli-dashboard-ehp: AtRiskReviewComplete (completion screen) — blocks n5z
2. broccoli-dashboard-n5z: AtRiskReviewModal (Vaul drawer shell + once-per-day gate) — blocks cjo, blocked by ehp
3. broccoli-dashboard-cjo: AppPage integration (mount modal, SW postMessage listener) — final wiring, blocked by ehp + n5z

The feature is 0% user-visible until cjo lands. ehp → n5z → cjo is the critical path.

PUSH NOTIFICATION PATH (parallel, not required for local testing):
4. broccoli-dashboard-a2b: SW deep-link (AT_RISK_REVIEW notification click → /app?intent=review or postMessage)

TESTING WITHOUT PUSH:
Once ehp+n5z+cjo land, the modal can be manually triggered via localStorage manipulation or URL param without needing the push notification path.
title: AtRiskReview: minimum shippable feature checklist (critical path to first use)

description:
In send-review-reminders.ts the sendPush call has data: { type: 'AT_RISK_REVIEW', tag: 'at-risk-review' }. The tag field inside data is redundant and potentially confusing.

WHY: The service worker (broccoli-dashboard-a2b, now closed) computes the notification tag itself from data.data.type — the SW never reads data.data.tag. Having a 'tag' key floating inside the push data payload looks like it does something, but it doesn't.

FILE: src/server/instrumentation/cron/scripts/send-review-reminders.ts

CHANGE: line ~92, update sendPush data payload:
  // Before:
  data: { type: 'AT_RISK_REVIEW', tag: 'at-risk-review' },
  // After:
  data: { type: 'AT_RISK_REVIEW' },

Minor cleanup — avoids confusion when reading payloads in DevTools or logs.
title: AtRiskReview gcy: drop redundant tag field from push data payload

close_reason: Implemented /api/push/resubscribe route in src/app/api/push/resubscribe/route.ts. Route authenticates, validates body, updates existing subscription by oldEndpoint+userId or upserts a new one if not found. Committed as fcff5dd on branch review-cards.
description:
Both public/sw-push.js and src/sw-custom.js call POST /api/push/resubscribe in their pushsubscriptionchange handler (added in broccoli-dashboard-zf8, commit 8183a0a), but that API route does not exist. Only /api/push/subscribe, /api/push/unsubscribe, and /api/push/send are present under src/app/api/push/.

When a browser fires pushsubscriptionchange (e.g. after a subscription expires and the browser auto-renews it), the SW will POST to /api/push/resubscribe and receive a 404. The resubscription key material will never reach the DB, so future push notifications to that device will fail with 410 Gone.

REQUIRED IMPLEMENTATION:
File: src/app/api/push/resubscribe/route.ts

The route should:
1. Authenticate via auth() — reject 401 if no session
2. Parse body: { oldEndpoint: string, newSubscription: { endpoint, keys: { p256dh, auth } } }
3. Validate all fields present
4. Find the existing PushSubscription by oldEndpoint (WHERE endpoint = oldEndpoint AND userId = session.user.id)
5. If found: update it — set endpoint, p256dh, auth to new values
6. If NOT found: create a new PushSubscription (browser may have lost the old record)
7. Return 200 { success: true }

The logic is very similar to /api/push/subscribe/route.ts (which already handles upsert by endpoint) — it can be modelled closely on that file.

NOTE: The pushsubscriptionchange event is fired by Chrome/Edge when a push subscription is about to expire and the browser renews it automatically. Without this handler + route, subscriptions silently expire and push stops working for that device without any user action.
title: /api/push/resubscribe route is missing — pushsubscriptionchange handler will 404

description:
From AT_RISK_REVIEW_PLAN.md Open Questions / Future Enhancements section.

Android Chrome supports an 'image' field in showNotification() that displays a large preview image in the notification tray. Including the item type icon URL would make the daily review reminder more visual and contextually recognisable.

CHANGE NEEDED:
1. In send-review-reminders.ts, include a representative item's icon URL in the push data payload.
   The icon URL can be derived from the at-risk item's itemType (e.g. /icons/{itemType.id}.png or whatever the existing icon URL scheme is).
2. In public/sw-push.js and src/sw-custom.js, read data.data.image and pass it to showNotification as the 'image' field.

NOTES:
- The 'image' field is only supported on Android Chrome. iOS/desktop ignore it gracefully.
- If no icon URL is available, omit the field entirely (don't pass undefined/null).
- The existing icon URL pattern for item types should be checked before implementing.
title: AtRiskReview: rich notification image — include item type icon URL in push payload

close_reason: Duplicate of broccoli-dashboard-97l (remove redundant tag field from push data payload in gcy/send-review-reminders.ts).
description: In the send-review-reminders.ts spec (broccoli-dashboard-gcy), the notificationService.sendPush call includes data: { type: 'AT_RISK_REVIEW', tag: 'at-risk-review' }. The 'tag' inside data is redundant — 'tag' is a notification option (set at the top level of showNotification), not a data field consumed by any app code. The service worker (a2b) sets the tag from data.data.type, not data.data.tag. When implementing gcy, drop the tag from data and only keep type: 'AT_RISK_REVIEW': data: { type: 'AT_RISK_REVIEW' }. Minor cleanup to avoid confusion when reading the payload in DevTools.
title: AtRiskReview(gcy): remove redundant tag field from push data payload

description: From AT_RISK_REVIEW_PLAN.md Open Questions section. Allow swiping up on a card to snooze that specific item for 1 day rather than adding it back into the next session's full queue. Track per-item snooze in localStorage keyed by item ID with a date, e.g. broccoli:atRiskReview:snooze:{itemId} = ISO date string. In useAtRiskReview, filter out items whose snooze date matches today before building the queue. close_reason: Added autoFocus?: boolean prop to BottomDrawerProps, forwarded to Drawer.Root (default false), and passed autoFocus={true} from AtRiskReviewModal. Committed in d4f2073.
description:
Vaul's Drawer.Root has autoFocus=false by default. When autoFocus=false, Vaul's Content.onOpenAutoFocus handler calls e.preventDefault(), which suppresses the browser's native autoFocus behavior on child elements. This means the autoFocus={isTop} prop added to the Eaten button in AtRiskReviewCard (broccoli-dashboard-4fk) will NOT actually move focus to the button when the drawer opens.

EVIDENCE:
In node_modules/vaul/dist/index.mjs:
  onOpenAutoFocus: (e) => {
    onOpenAutoFocus?.(e);
    if (!autoFocus) {
      e.preventDefault();  // <-- this suppresses child autoFocus
    }
  }

And Drawer.Root default: autoFocus = false

FIX (two options, pick one):
1. Pass autoFocus={true} to BottomDrawer's Drawer.Root in src/components/BottomDrawer.tsx — but this changes focus behavior globally for ALL uses of BottomDrawer.
   
2. Better: pass autoFocus prop through BottomDrawer to Drawer.Root only when needed. The AtRiskReviewModal already uses renderHeader={...} custom prop pattern, so BottomDrawer already accepts custom props. Add an optional autoFocus prop to BottomDrawerProps that forwards to Drawer.Root. Then pass autoFocus={true} from AtRiskReviewModal.

FILE TO MODIFY: src/components/BottomDrawer.tsx
- Add autoFocus?: boolean to BottomDrawerProps interface (default false to preserve current behavior)
- Forward it: <Drawer.Root autoFocus={autoFocus} ...>

FILE TO MODIFY: src/components/core/AtRiskReview/AtRiskReviewModal.tsx
- Pass autoFocus={true} to BottomDrawer

NOTE: autoFocus={isTop} on the Eaten button (broccoli-dashboard-4fk) is still the right pattern — it ensures the correct element receives focus once Vaul stops suppressing it. Both fixes are needed together.

DISCOVERED WHILE: Working broccoli-dashboard-4fk — noticed Vaul's source prevents this from working without the Drawer.Root-level prop.
title: AtRiskReviewModal: autoFocus on Eaten button is suppressed by Vaul's onOpenAutoFocus override

close_reason: Duplicate of broccoli-dashboard-97l (remove redundant tag field from push data payload in gcy/send-review-reminders.ts).
description: In the send-review-reminders.ts spec (broccoli-dashboard-gcy), the notificationService.sendPush call includes data: { type: 'AT_RISK_REVIEW', tag: 'at-risk-review' }. The tag inside data is redundant — tag is a notification option set at the top level of showNotification, not a data field consumed by any app code. The service worker (a2b) reads data.data.type to set the notification tag, not data.data.tag. When implementing gcy, use data: { type: 'AT_RISK_REVIEW' } only. Minor cleanup to avoid confusion when reading the push payload in DevTools.
title: AtRiskReview(gcy): remove redundant tag field from push data payload

description:
Phase 5 item 14 from AT_RISK_REVIEW_PLAN.md. After a swipe is committed (handleDragEnd fires and the card animates out), call navigator.vibrate([50]) to give a short tactile pulse on mobile.

FILE: src/components/core/AtRiskReview/AtRiskReviewCard.tsx

WHERE: In the handleDragEnd callback, after the decision threshold is crossed and before/after the onDecision(decision) call:
  if ('vibrate' in navigator) {
    navigator.vibrate(50); // short tactile pulse
  }

NOTES:
- Guard with 'vibrate' in navigator — not supported on iOS Safari, will silently no-op.
- Do NOT vibrate on a cancelled drag (when the card snaps back). Only vibrate on a committed swipe.
- The vibration should be subtle (50ms). Avoid long patterns that feel aggressive.
- This is mobile-only by product design (the modal only renders when isMobile === true), so no additional device gate is needed beyond the navigator.vibrate feature check.
title: AtRiskReview: haptic feedback via navigator.vibrate on swipe commit (mobile only)

close_reason: Duplicate of broccoli-dashboard-46y (same bug, same fix). Fix committed in same commit as 46y.
description:
BUG: AtRiskReviewModal initialises isOpen from shouldOpen at render time, but shouldOpen depends on isMobile (MediaQueriesContext) and the at-risk query — both false/empty during SSR hydration. Because isOpen is useState(shouldOpen) with no useEffect to re-sync, the modal never auto-opens on first load even when all conditions are met.

SYMPTOM: On mobile, modal should auto-open once per day when at-risk items exist. In practice it silently does nothing because isOpen was set to false before isMobile resolved.

ROOT CAUSE:
  const shouldOpen = isMobile and session.shouldShow and (review.isLoading or !review.isEmpty);
  const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(shouldOpen);  // initialised once, never re-synced

SECONDARY: The null early-return guard fires before effects mount, so the SW postMessage listener (setIsOpen(true)) never registers when shouldOpen starts false. OPEN_AT_RISK_REVIEW postMessages silently drop on initial load.

FIX: Add a useEffect that syncs shouldOpen to isOpen when shouldOpen first becomes true. Also move the null guard below the effect registrations so the SW postMessage listener always registers.

FILE: src/components/core/AtRiskReview/AtRiskReviewModal.tsx
title: AtRiskReviewModal: isOpen never syncs to shouldOpen after hydration

description:
Phase 5 item from AT_RISK_REVIEW_PLAN.md + cjo (broccoli-dashboard-cjo) PART 4. The feature is wired up but these edge cases haven't been manually validated.

TEST MATRIX — document findings in issue comments:

1. 0 at-risk items → modal must never open (isEmpty=true from start, gate fails before BottomDrawer mounts)
2. 1 item → single card shows, no stack behind it; queue.slice(0,3) with 1 item = 1 card; completing it goes straight to AtRiskReviewComplete
3. Slow network → skeleton card (animate-pulse placeholder) shows while query isLoading=true; modal opens immediately without waiting for data
4. User closes mid-deck SAME day → localStorage NOT marked complete; modal re-opens with remaining queue if they reopen the app that day
5. User closes mid-deck NEXT day → localStorage date mismatch → modal opens fresh with full new queue
6. Push notification tap when app is CLOSED → ?intent=review lands on /app, AtRiskReviewModal mounts, setForceOpen(true) triggers open
7. Push notification tap when app is OPEN → SW postMessage OPEN_AT_RISK_REVIEW received, modal opens in-place without navigation
8. 'Remind me later' (X button) → modal closes, NOT marked complete, will re-open next time app loads
9. 'Don't show today' link → modal closes AND session.markComplete() called, won't show again today

HOW TO TRIGGER manually:
- Clear localStorage key 'broccoli:atRiskReview:lastCompletedDate' to reset gate
- Use DevTools > Application > Service Workers > Push to simulate push notification
- Use DevTools > Network > Throttle to test slow network skeleton

NOTE: The auto-open hydration bug was fixed in broccoli-dashboard-46y (commit a3e88eb). The OPEN_AT_RISK_REVIEW postMessage null-guard drop was also fixed in that commit.
title: AtRiskReview: manual edge-case testing matrix (0 items, 1 item, slow network, mid-deck dismiss)

description:
After a card is swiped, show a floating 'Undo' toast via sonner (already installed) for 3 seconds. If the user taps Undo before it disappears, reverse the last decision: remove the result from review.results, push the item back to the front of the queue, and fire a compensating updateItem mutation to restore its previous status/percentConsumed.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- In useAtRiskReview, expose an undo() function alongside advance(). undo() only works if results.length > 0.
- The toast should be triggered from AtRiskReviewDeck after onDecision fires successfully. Use sonner's toast() with an action button.
- Only support undoing the MOST RECENT decision (single-level undo). Calling undo twice in a row would undo the same action twice -- guard against this.
- 'skipped' decisions should also be undoable (puts item back at front of queue, no mutation needed).
- The compensating mutation for 'eaten' should restore percentConsumed and status to the item's original values (captured at queue-init time in useAtRiskReview).

DEPENDS ON: broccoli-dashboard-8d4 (AtRiskReviewDeck must exist), broccoli-dashboard-479 (useAtRiskReview hook must exist).
title: AtRiskReview: undo last swipe (sonner snackbar)

description:
public/sw-push.js and src/sw-custom.js were intended to stay in sync (per broccoli-dashboard-a2b notes) but they have already diverged before the AT_RISK_REVIEW changes in a2b were applied.

CURRENT DIFFERENCES:
- sw-custom.js has a pushsubscriptionchange handler; sw-push.js does not
- Log prefixes differ ('[SW Push]' vs '[Service Worker]')
- notificationclick handler: sw-custom.js checks client.url.includes(urlToOpen) (bug: urlToOpen = '/app', so if app is at /app?intent=review it would still match, but the check is subtly different from sw-push.js which checks client.url.includes('/app'))
- sw-custom.js has a comment explaining itemId navigation that sw-push.js lacks

RISK: When broccoli-dashboard-a2b is worked, changes must be applied to both files. If the person working a2b doesn't notice these existing differences they may accidentally overwrite the pushsubscriptionchange handler in sw-custom.js or introduce new divergence.

RECOMMENDED ACTION: Before working a2b, reconcile the two files so they share identical logic for push/notificationclick handlers, keeping the pushsubscriptionchange handler from sw-custom.js as the canonical version (sw-push.js should gain it). Then apply a2b changes once to both.

FILES: public/sw-push.js, src/sw-custom.js
title: Service worker files have diverged (sw-push.js vs sw-custom.js)
